The Future of WebAssembly in 2026

Written by Dr. Maria Chen — Published March 15, 2026

WebAssembly is no longer just a browser technology. This article explores how WASM is reshaping server-side computing, edge functions, and embedded systems.

The Browser Era

When WebAssembly launched in 2017, it was designed as a compilation target for C/C++ in browsers. Fast forward to 2026, and the landscape looks entirely different.

Beyond the Browser

WASI (WebAssembly System Interface) has matured into a robust standard. Companies like Fastly, Cloudflare, and Fermyon are running WASM workloads at scale on the edge.

What Comes Next

Component Model, shared-nothing linking, and WASM GC are the next frontiers. Expect major announcements at WasmCon 2026.

Rust and WASM: A Perfect Match Edge Computing Trends
